notes.note: C. Asinius Pollio, the future soldier, orator, and historian, then a young man in his twenties; this is his earliest appearance in the Ciceronian correspondence, as a courier-witness who had 'presided over' the Egyptian business at Rome.

notes.anchor: the recollection of my own times
notes.note: Cicero's own exile of 58 BC, from which he had returned only the previous autumn; he offers it as the consoling parallel to Lentulus's reverses, the lesser injury mirroring the greater.
A Greek proverb (ek hapalon onuchon, 'from tender nails') meaning 'from earliest childhood.' Cicero quotes it in Greek to say he has known Lentulus's true character from boyhood.
